institute.meta.title: WPA Institute · Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies
institute.meta.description: World Protocol Academy Institute — a specialised institute for protocol, diplomacy, public communication and security studies. Research, analytics, benchmark, lectures by practitioners.
institute.topbar.text: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies
institute.hero.title: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies
institute.hero.lead: World Protocol Academy relie la théorie académique à la pratique professionnelle, à l'éthique et aux résultats mesurables.
institute.hero.formula_text: Theory + practice + ethics + measurability = a living school for protocol, diplomacy, public communication and security.
institute.identity.label: Identité institutionnelle
institute.identity.title: World Protocol Academy en tant qu'institut spécialisé
institute.identity.subtitle: A specialised academic and professional platform
institute.identity.p1: WPA is a specialised academic and professional platform for research, training, applied practice, certification discipline, analytics and institutional benchmark models within the Institute's domain.
institute.identity.p2: WPA does not present itself as a university and does not claim state accreditation. The Institute develops its own specialised methodology, professional standards and analytical indicators.
institute.identity.p3: The Institute operates as a living school: theory, verifiable sources, real practice and measurable results — without academic or legal overreach.
institute.charter.label: Charte de l'Institut
institute.charter.title: Six piliers de la Charte
institute.charter.lead: The Charter articulates the foundational principles under which the Institute operates — no negotiation, no concessions, no special cases.
institute.charter.p1.title: Academic integrity
institute.charter.p1.text: All research and educational materials are original WPA creations or correctly attributed sources.
institute.charter.p2.title: Professional measurability
institute.charter.p2.text: Every programme, certificate and analytical indicator has clear criteria, transparent methodology and verifiable evidence.
institute.charter.p3.title: Ethical independence
institute.charter.p3.text: WPA does not permit payment for a better result, does not perform accreditation and does not award state recognition.
institute.charter.p4.title: Discipline of language
institute.charter.p4.text: The Institute does not use words such as “partner”, “accredited” or “endorsed” without a separate written agreement.
institute.charter.p5.title: Transparent sources
institute.charter.p5.text: A 9-field citation schema, openness of methodology, and the right of correction for the institutions being measured.
institute.charter.p6.title: Founder's doctrine
institute.charter.p6.text: “Negotiation is optional. Protocol is absolute.” — the operating logic of all Institute activities.
institute.domains.label: Institute domains
institute.domains.title: Six domains of specialisation
institute.domains.d1.title: Protocol studies
institute.domains.d1.text: State, diplomatic, institutional, ceremonial and international protocol.
institute.domains.d2.title: Diplomacy and international relations
institute.domains.d2.text: Diplomatic practice, international relations, public diplomacy and international law.
institute.domains.d3.title: Public communication
institute.domains.d3.text: Public speaking, public relations, strategic communication and institutional reputation.
institute.domains.d4.title: Security studies
institute.domains.d4.text: Security studies, defence diplomacy, crisis procedures and public events.
institute.domains.d5.title: Research and benchmark analysis
institute.domains.d5.text: Working papers, case studies, policy briefs, indices, methodologies and annual reports.
institute.domains.d6.title: Practical professional wisdom
institute.domains.d6.text: WPA values both academic sources and long-standing documented practical experience.
institute.research.label: Piliers de recherche
institute.research.title: Quatre directions principales de recherche
institute.research.lead: L'Institut structure ses working papers, monographies, notes de politique et analyses de benchmark autour de quatre piliers : protocole, diplomatie, sécurité et communication publique.
institute.research.r1.title: Théorie et pratique du protocole
institute.research.r1.text: Historical development, comparative analysis, ceremonial logic, precedent, precedence, flag protocol, order of seniority, symbols of the state.
institute.research.r2.title: Diplomatie et relations internationales
institute.research.r2.text: Bilateral relations, multilateral frameworks, public diplomacy, defence diplomacy, conference management, negotiation methods.
institute.research.r3.title: Sécurité et diplomatie de défense
institute.research.r3.text: Public event security, crisis coordination, VIP protection, incident protocol, protection of diplomatic personnel.
institute.research.r4.title: Communication publique et réputation institutionnelle
institute.research.r4.text: Strategic communication, public relations, media relations, crisis communication, digital reputation, institutional narrative.
institute.analytics.label: Analytics and measurability
institute.analytics.title: Centre WPA d'analytique, de mesurabilité et d'indices institutionnels
institute.analytics.lead: WPA is developing a specialised analytics centre to track, compare and evaluate institutions, academies, universities, programmes, experts and research activities in the fields of protocol, diplomacy, public communication, public relations, security studies and communication skills.
institute.analytics.measured.title: What will be measured
institute.analytics.measured.i2: citation impact and visibility where verifiable;
institute.analytics.measured.i3: programmes, teaching content and certification discipline;
institute.analytics.measured.i4: annual conferences, simulations and applied research;
institute.analytics.measured.i5: practical professional impact and knowledge transfer;
institute.analytics.measured.i6: international cooperation, public communication, digital transparency.
institute.analytics.notwhat.title: What the WPA Index is NOT
institute.analytics.notwhat.i2: it is not a university ranking in the classical sense;
institute.analytics.notwhat.i3: it is not a professional licence;
institute.analytics.notwhat.i4: it does not replace Scopus, Web of Science, ORCID, Google Scholar;
institute.analytics.notwhat.i5: it does not permit payment for a better result;
institute.analytics.notwhat.i6: it is not a final assessment without the possibility of correction.
institute.analytics.index.title: Institute Index — four dimensions
institute.analytics.index.d1.title: Research and publications
institute.analytics.index.d1.text: Working papers, books, articles, citation visibility, ORCID and Google Scholar evidence.
institute.analytics.index.d2.title: Programmes and certification
institute.analytics.index.d2.text: Programme structure, learning outcomes, assessment discipline, verification.
institute.analytics.index.d3.title: Professional practice
institute.analytics.index.d3.text: Documented practitioner activity, applied research, real-world institutional deployment.
institute.analytics.index.d4.title: Transparency and cooperation
institute.analytics.index.d4.text: Publication openness, international cooperation, digital institutional clarity.
institute.analytics.index.note: The category weights are indicative, not final. Final weights will be published in the public Institute Index v1.0 methodology following review by the Academic Advisory Board.
institute.analytics.disclaimer: WPA indices are specialised analytical tools. Final assessments are based on publicly verifiable evidence, methodological transparency, and, where necessary, human review by WPA and external advisers.
institute.methodology.label: Methodological groups
institute.methodology.title: Catégories pour une comparaison équitable
institute.methodology.lead: WPA ne mélange pas dans un seul tableau approximatif les petites académies de protocole et les grandes universités.
institute.methodology.gA.name: Protocol / etiquette / diplomatic-protocol academies
institute.methodology.gA.examples: Protocol Academy of Macedonia, IEPA London, Accademia del Cerimoniale, Protocol School of Washington
institute.methodology.gB.name: Diplomatic academies and government training institutes
institute.methodology.gB.examples: Diplomatic Academy of Vienna, Bulgarian Diplomatic Institute, Diplomacy Academy Türkiye, Clingendael Institute
institute.methodology.gC.name: Universities / faculties of diplomacy, international relations, law, political science
institute.methodology.gC.examples: China Foreign Affairs University, Sciences Po, CEDS France, College of Europe
institute.methodology.gD.name: Foreign service institutes
institute.methodology.gD.examples: US FSI, Instituto Rio Branco, ISEN Argentina, Foreign Service Institute of India, FSI Indonesia
institute.methodology.gE.name: Institutes of cultural and intercultural diplomacy
institute.methodology.gE.examples: Institute for Cultural Diplomacy, Anna Lindh Foundation, British Council Cultural Relations
institute.methodology.gF.name: Training centres of international organisations
institute.methodology.gF.examples: UN Staff College, OSCE Academy, NATO School, EU College of Europe (Bruges/Natolin)
institute.methodology.gG.name: Public relations and communication studies
institute.methodology.gG.examples: USC Annenberg, NYU SPS, University of Amsterdam, NTU Wee Kim Wee School
institute.methodology.gH.name: Security, defence and strategic studies
institute.methodology.gH.examples: NATO Defense College, King's College War Studies, Georgetown SSP, ANU SDSC
institute.methodology.gI.name: Globally prestigious benchmark institutions
institute.methodology.gI.examples: Georgetown SFS, Johns Hopkins SAIS, Harvard Kennedy School, Oxford, LSE
institute.benchmark.label: Global benchmark map
institute.benchmark.title: Carte mondiale des institutions de référence
institute.benchmark.lead: The map is not a final ranking. It is an initial analytical map of selected institutions that serve as reference points for the development of the WPA methodology.
institute.benchmark.r1.title: North America
institute.benchmark.r2.title: Latin America
institute.benchmark.r3.title: Europe
institute.benchmark.r4.title: Protocol academies
institute.benchmark.r5.title: Asia and the Middle East
institute.benchmark.r6.title: Security and Oceania
institute.benchmark.disclaimer: For public use WPA presents only verified names and clean labels. Entries with incorrect spellings, duplicates or inaccurate countries will not be published.
institute.opc.title: First International Conference on Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Relations and Security
institute.opc.text1: OPC 2026 (Ohrid Protocol Conference) is the Institute's flagship event — a gathering of academic researchers, protocol officers, diplomats, security experts and communicators from over 17 countries.
institute.opc.text2: The conference connects theory with practice, academic authority with field experience, and the regional perspective with global standards.
institute.practitioners.label: Practitioner lecture series
institute.practitioners.title: Théorie liée à la pratique
institute.practitioners.lead: The Institute invites experienced practitioners to deliver specialised lectures from their field. With written consent, lectures may be recorded and added to the Institute's educational archive for students, listeners, researchers and professionals.
institute.practitioners.c1.title: Protocol and diplomacy
institute.practitioners.c1.text: Heads of protocol, diplomats, former ambassadors, diplomatic officers, masters of ceremonies and experts on state and international events.
institute.practitioners.c2.title: Defence, security and military attachés
institute.practitioners.c2.text: Defence and military attachés, security coordinators, experts on public events, crisis procedures, institutional security and inter-institutional coordination.
institute.practitioners.c3.title: Public communication, media and PR
institute.practitioners.c3.text: Spokespersons, PR directors, journalists, editors, media advisers and experts on crisis communication, public speaking and institutional reputation.
institute.practitioners.c4.title: Visual diplomacy and documentation
institute.practitioners.c4.text: Official photographers, camera crews, visual documentarians, translators, interpreters and professionals who shape the public image of diplomatic and protocol events.
institute.practitioners.pathway.title: Practitioner Pathway — five steps
institute.practitioners.pathway.s1.title: Invitation
institute.practitioners.pathway.s1.text: The Institute identifies an experienced practitioner on the basis of a publicly documented position and role.
institute.practitioners.pathway.s2.title: Agreement
institute.practitioners.pathway.s2.text: Written consent is signed for the topic, scope and the right to record and archive.
institute.practitioners.pathway.s3.title: Lecture
institute.practitioners.pathway.s3.text: Lecture in a structured format: 45–60 minutes of presentation + 15 minutes of Q&A with participants.
institute.practitioners.pathway.s4.title: Archive
institute.practitioners.pathway.s4.text: The lecture is added to the Institute's educational archive with clear attribution and metadata.
institute.practitioners.pathway.s5.title: Recognition
institute.practitioners.pathway.s5.text: The practitioner receives Institute recognition for a documented professional contribution — without an academic title.
institute.practitioners.disclaimer: Participation as a practitioner lecturer of the Institute does not constitute an academic title, state accreditation or professional licence. It is recognition of documented professional experience and contribution to the educational mission of the Institute.
institute.aab.label: Academic Advisory Board
institute.aab.title: AAB — independent external advisory
institute.aab.subtitle: Structure and role
institute.aab.p1: The Academic Advisory Board (AAB) is an independent advisory body composed of distinguished academic and professional figures within the Institute's domain: professors, former diplomats, heads of protocol, security experts and communication specialists.
institute.aab.p2: The AAB reviews the methodology of the Institute Index, audits the quality of working papers, advises on research priorities and provides an independent opinion on cases involving potential conflict of interest.
institute.aab.p3: The AAB is not an operational body — the Institute is led by the Director. The AAB is the advisory conscience: a check on quality, not on operations.
institute.publications.label: Public outputs
institute.publications.title: Publications et productions analytiques de l'Institut
institute.publications.s1.title: WPA Working Papers
institute.publications.s1.text: The first four WPA Working Papers are published as public Zenodo DOI records: WP-001, WP-002, WP-003 and WP-004.
institute.publications.s2.title: WPA Protocol Studies Review
institute.publications.s2.text: Specialised analyses from protocol and diplomatic practice.
institute.publications.s3.title: WPA Policy Briefs
institute.publications.s3.text: Short applied recommendations for institutions and professionals.
institute.publications.s4.title: WPA Case Studies
institute.publications.s4.text: Documented case studies from the Institute's domain.
institute.publications.s5.title: WPA Annual Report
institute.publications.s5.text: Annual institutional review of activities, results and future directions.
institute.publications.s6.title: WPA Global Benchmark
institute.publications.s6.text: Specialised benchmark analysis for protocol, diplomacy, communication and security.
institute.cta.title: The Institute is building a serious, modern and internationally oriented academy — with structure, dignity and a clear direction.
institute.footer.about_text: Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies. Institutional formula: theory + practice + ethics + measurability.
institute.footer.bottom: © 2026 World Protocol Academy · Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication and Security Studies · Negotiation is optional. Protocol is absolute.

institute.trust.title: Couche de confiance, provenance et corrections
institute.trust.lead: WPA utilise une couche de confiance publique pour la transparence, le droit de correction, les demandes de retrait, la transparence IA, la provenance du contenu et la discipline méthodologique.
institute.trust.li1: right of correction for institutions and individuals;
institute.trust.li2: public disclaimer for Institute Index and benchmark materials;
institute.trust.li3: AI transparency and human review of content;
institute.trust.li5: clear separation between analysis, opinion, index and accreditation.
institute.trust.not_li2: it is not a legal judgment;
institute.trust.not_li3: it is not a professional licence;
institute.trust.not_li4: it is not a paid mechanism for a better score;
institute.trust.not_li5: it is not public disclosure of private data.
institute.index.title: WPA Global Institutions Index & outils de benchmark
institute.index.lead: Passerelle publique vers WPA Master List, méthodologie de benchmark et processus de correction pour les institutions du protocole, de la diplomatie, de la communication publique et des études de sécurité.
institute.index.note: WPA Index is not state accreditation, not a professional licence, and does not replace Scopus, Web of Science, ORCID, Google Scholar or national accreditation bodies.
institute.benchmark.inst.anu_strategic_a: ANU Strategic and Defence Studies Centre
institute.author.title: Bureau WPA de soutien aux auteurs et préparation à l'indexation
institute.author.li2: improvement of abstract, title and keywords;
institute.author.li3: review of structure, methodology and citations;
institute.author.h_not: Ce que WPA ne fait pas
institute.author.not_li1: does not guarantee publication in Scopus or Web of Science;
institute.author.not_li3: does not sell faster peer review;
institute.author.not_li4: does not manipulate citations or H-index;
institute.author.not_li6: does not claim indexing without official confirmation.

institute.ai.c6.label: Éthique
institute.ai.c6.title: Human Review & AI Transparency
institute.author.lead: WPA fournit un soutien éthique aux auteurs, chercheurs et praticiens.
institute.author.note: WPA does not guarantee publication in Scopus, Web of Science, DOAJ or any external index. WPA provides ethical author support, manuscript readiness, methodological review, citation integrity, AI transparency, predatory journal screening and guidance for legitimate academic publishing.
institute.journal_section.lead: World Protocol Academy's specialised international interdisciplinary journal on protocol, diplomacy, public communication, public relations, security studies and communicology.
institute.journal.note: Submission is free. Peer review is free for authors. A symbolic fee applies only on acceptance, with waivers available. No payment for acceptance, no paid ranking, no guaranteed publication.
institute.student.lead: WPA développe un espace étudiant assisté par IA pour l'apprentissage du protocole, de la diplomatie, de la communication publique et de la culture de sécurité.
institute.student.note: Student Desk Beta is not an officially completed certification platform. All final certificates, records and student data will be managed only through a dedicated privacy, consent and security procedure.
institute.ai.lead: World Protocol Academy suit les derniers outils IA.
institute.ai.c1.text: WPA will use AI video generation and editing to support educational scenarios, public communication, course previews, protocol simulations and short explanatory videos — always with human control and clear labelling.
institute.ai.c2.text: Advanced reasoning and long-form drafting models are used to prepare drafts, structures, research maps, comparative analyses and working documents. Final responsibility always remains human and editorial.
institute.ai.c3.text: AI audio tools can help in preparing voiceovers, audiobook chapters, lecture narration, multilingual summaries and training materials, respecting consent, attribution and institutional dignity.
institute.ai.c4.text: WPA will develop a trust layer for AI-generated or AI-assisted content through provenance records, disclosure notes, content credentials and correction pathways, without misrepresenting content origin.
institute.ai.c5.text: WPAWS will be developed as an institutional workspace for ideas, papers, books, courses, presentations, videos, journal submissions, index workflows and public communication packages.
institute.ai.c6.text: AI can help in the process but must not fabricate facts, citations, consents, institutional links or academic status. Every public piece of content goes through human review.
institute.ai.note: WPA does not claim AI systems are infallible, nor that AI can replace academic, editorial or institutional responsibility. AI is used as a support tool, not as a standalone authority. All sensitive content, public statements, index scores, certificates and journal decisions require human review.
